text:
There was a young fox that lived near a forest clearing. Every day after school, he would sneak out to the edge just enough to peer into the ancient trees. There lived a wise old owl whose knowledge extended beyond birdsong. One evening, as the young fox gazed intently at a particularly gnarled owl perched on a branch, he heard a faint song.

That night, as stars twinkled overhead, the fox became obsessed with finding that tune. He sang it into an old horn he found tucked beneath a mossy oak. To his amazement, the owl showed up at dawn, humming along.

Days turned into weeks, and the owl taught the fox the secrets of nature. It shared songs from countless moons. The fox learned that each song held a story about the forest, a memory or feeling retained through melody.

When winter came, they sang together by the fireplace when the young fox couldn't leave his snowy home. Years passed, and the fox grew old.
